text: The surgeon uses four robotic arms: Arm one with a ProGrasp, Arm two with a Maryland dissector, Arm three with the camera, and Arm four with hook cautery, to retract and manipulate tissues in a standard manner.
The surgeon dissects to achieve the critical view of safety, utilizing indocyanine green dye to enhance visualization of the biliary anatomy.

The surgeon identifies an aberrant artery running laterally, adjacent to the hook cautery, and leaves it intact as it extends towards the cystic plate. The critical view of safety is obtained, with visibility through the opening in the posterior wall of the right hepatic lobe.
The surgeon uses round-tip scissors to peel the gallbladder off the cystic plate, performing a straightforward cholecystectomy.
